releaseStatus: Standards coverage, instructional evidence, progress summaries, and reporting comments are connected.

defaultStandards.subject: Reading Literature
defaultStandards.title: Ask and answer questions about a text
defaultStandards.description: Students use key details to demonstrate understanding.

defaultStandards.subject: Reading Informational
defaultStandards.title: Identify a main topic and supporting details
defaultStandards.description: Students explain what a text is mostly about and identify supporting information.

defaultStandards.subject: Foundational Reading
defaultStandards.title: Read with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression
defaultStandards.description: Students read connected text with accuracy, phrasing, punctuation, and expression.

defaultStandards.subject: Writing
defaultStandards.title: Write an explanatory response
defaultStandards.description: Students introduce a topic, provide information, and create a concluding statement.

defaultStandards.subject: Language / Grammar
defaultStandards.title: Apply grade-level language conventions
defaultStandards.description: Students use grammar, capitalization, punctuation, and spelling in context.

defaultStandards.subject: Mathematics
defaultStandards.title: Represent and solve grade-level problems
defaultStandards.description: Students select strategies, explain thinking, and check solutions.

defaultStandards.subject: Science
defaultStandards.title: Use evidence to explain a science concept
defaultStandards.description: Students observe, record, and explain patterns or cause-and-effect relationships.

defaultStandards.subject: Classroom Habits
defaultStandards.title: Demonstrate independence and responsible classroom habits
defaultStandards.description: Students follow routines, use materials responsibly, and complete tasks with growing independence.

Secure: [Student Name] consistently demonstrates understanding of [skill] and applies the skill independently.
[Student Name] is secure with [skill] and uses evidence or strategies to explain thinking.
Approaching: [Student Name] is approaching proficiency with [skill] and benefits from occasional prompting or guided practice.
[Student Name] shows growing understanding of [skill] and is working toward independent application.
Developing: [Student Name] is developing understanding of [skill] and benefits from explicit modeling, guided practice, and feedback.
[Student Name] continues to build [skill] through small-group instruction and repeated opportunities to practice.
Beginning: [Student Name] is beginning to develop [skill] and currently requires targeted support and frequent teacher guidance.
[Student Name] benefits from visual supports, oral rehearsal, and reduced steps while learning [skill].
Extending: [Student Name] exceeds current expectations in [skill] and applies learning to more complex tasks.
[Student Name] demonstrates advanced understanding of [skill] and explains thinking with strong evidence.
